name: BONE ISLAND
definition: ENOSTOSIS = ENDOSTEOMA = COMPACT ISLAND FOCAL SCLEROSIS = SCLEROTIC BONE ISLAND CALCIFIED MEDULLARY DEFECT focal lesion of densely sclerotic (compact) bone nesting within spongiosa

demographics.Age: any age (mostly 20-80 years of age); grows more rapidly in children
demographics.Histo: nest of lamellar compacted bone with haversian system embedded within medullary
demographics.Size: usually 2-10 mm; lesion > 2 cm in longest axis = giant bone island

dominant_finding: low signal intensity at T1WI and T2WI
distribution: NO involvement of cortex / radiolucencies / periosteal reaction
discriminating_features: round / oval / oblong solitary osteoblastic lesion with abrupt transition to surrounding normal
"brush border" = "thorny radiations" = sharply demarcated margins with feathery
long axis of bone island parallels long axis of bone
may demonstrate slow growth / decrease in size (32%)
NO involvement of cortex / radiolucencies / periosteal reaction

general: round / oval / oblong solitary osteoblastic lesion with abrupt transition to surrounding normal
"brush border" = "thorny radiations" = sharply demarcated margins with feathery
long axis of bone island parallels long axis of bone
may demonstrate slow growth / decrease in size (32%)
NO involvement of cortex / radiolucencies / periosteal reaction
MR: low signal intensity at T1WI and T2WI
NUC: may show activity on bone scan (< 10%), esp. if large

clinical.Prognosis: may increase to 8-12 cm over years (40%); may decrease / disappear
